Title: Identifying Candidate Genes for Grape (Vitis vinifera L.) Fruit Firmness through Genome-Wide Association Studies
Journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in pBWA(V)H-1300-GFP vector inferred from overexpression construct; exact promoter not explicitly stated
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major structural component of the primary cell wall that contributes mechanical strength and supports fruit firmness
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit peel and flesh cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Cellulose synthase-like D subpathway of cell wall polysaccharide synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Promotes synthesis of cellulose polysaccharide for cell wall formation from UDP-sugar precursors; exact in vivo sugar donor/product linkage not directly assayed in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point suggested but not formally established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cellulose accumulation and increases fruit peel and flesh firmness
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; candidate-gene collinearity/family analysis; heterologous overexpression in tomato; subcellular localization; cellulose quantification; fruit firmness measurement
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal growth conditions for transgenic tomato
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing and GWAS; Genome Sequence Archive PRJCA026367; NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA1226384
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression increased plant height 2.03-fold, altered main stem thickness, and significantly elongated fruit transverse and longitudinal diameters, indicating pleiotropic growth effects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified by integrating GWAS for firmness and cellulose content with CesA/Csl gene family and collinearity analyses; highlighted as likely dual regulator of cellulose synthesis and berry firmness
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvCslD5 (VIT_16s0039g02020) was prioritized from GWAS, CesA/Csl family identification, and collinearity analysis as a key cellulose-related candidate gene. 2. VvCslD5 protein localized to the plasma membrane. 3. Overexpression in tomato increased peel firmness by 1.81-fold and flesh firmness by 1.85-fold. 4. Overexpression also doubled cellulose content in peel and flesh, supporting a causal role in cellulose-mediated fruit firmness.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for breeding or engineering firmer grape berries with improved storage and transport traits by enhancing cellulose deposition; should be deployed with caution because of pleiotropic effects on vegetative growth and fruit morphology

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 120 grape genotypes for phenotype/GWAS; 153 grape genotypes for resequencing population
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides mechanical strength to the cell wall and contributes to berry firmness
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit peel and flesh cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose_synt domain; some VvCesA proteins also contain zf-UDP domain
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane predicted/consistent with localization of cellulose synthases discussed in paper
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes cellulose microfibril polymer for the cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: candidate key step in firmness-associated cellulose synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate cellulose biosynthetic genes associated with fruit firmness and cellulose content
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS candidate-gene mapping; gene family identification; domain verification
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing and GWAS; Genome Sequence Archive PRJCA026367; NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA1226384
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper states CesA was identified as a candidate class potentially regulating fruit firmness through cellulose biosynthesis; only VvCslD5 received direct functional validation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS implicated cellulose synthase genes as candidates for peel/flesh firmness and cellulose content. 2. CesA/Csl family analysis identified 67 VvCesA/Csl members in grape. 3. Cellulose synthase genes were highlighted as central components of the firmness-regulating cell wall network, but direct validation in this study focused on VvCslD5.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful candidate family for marker-assisted selection or gene editing to improve berry firmness via cellulose deposition, with priority on validated members such as VvCslD5
